action.hypothesis: The gated K=128 EF=16 model has now regressed at both 3.2e-3 and 8e-4, so the remaining clean learning-rate check is whether a more conservative signum step size of 4e-4 recovers a smoother optimization path and improves proxy FVU relative to the failed lower-LR probe.
action.summary: Keep the gated K=128 EF=16 architecture fixed and continue the gated stabilization sweep by testing LR=4e-4 in proxy.
action.self_review: This stays within the current best family and changes exactly one launcher-safe parameter in line with the exploitation guidance. The main risk is that the model is already under-updating and 4e-4 will be even worse, but it is the highest-priority untested LR in the remaining systematic sweep.
notes_to_memory: The gated EF=16 mainline has now failed at both higher and moderately lower LRs, so this round closes the remaining conservative LR bracket before moving to other single-parameter sweeps.
This round keeps the strongest validated gated architecture fixed at K=128 and tests only LR=4e-4 in proxy.
If LR=4e-4 also regresses, the next exploitation axis should shift from LR to gated-specific regularization such as AUXK_ALPHA rather than continuing to shrink step size blindly.
next_hypotheses: If LR=4e-4 improves proxy FVU materially, promote to full and compare against the current gated K=128 frontier.
If LR=4e-4 is also worse, begin a gated AUXK sweep with AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625 as the next stabilization probe.
If conservative LR sweeps all fail, treat optimization as bracketed and focus subsequent rounds on auxiliary regularization or expansion changes within gated rather than more LR-only tuning.
